div.head: The American Representative in Hungary (Schoenfeld) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 1026. In accordance with urtel 754, November 30,See footnote 81, p. 1318. I have informed Hungarian GovtNote of December 4, 1945, from the United States Mission in Hungary to the Hungarian Ministry for Foreign Affairs is quoted at length in Stephen Kertesz, “The Expulsion of the Germans from Hungary: A Study in Postwar Diplomacy”, The Review of Politics, vol. 15, 1953, p. 193. substance of Dept’s 310 to Warsaw and expressed gratification of this Mission that Hungarians have already accepted Potsdam principles on population transfers in Foreign Office note November 5 transmitted with my despatch 546 November 14 and copied to Berlin. We added that it was, therefore, to be expected transfer of Germans from Hungary would proceed in orderly and humane manner.

General KeyMaj. Gen. William S. Key, Chief, United States Section, Allied Control Commission for Hungary. has been asked to inform colleagues on ACC (Allied Control Commission) of substance your 310 to Warsaw and advised of our communication to Hungarian Govt.
